description: errorBoundary §19.6.5 priority #2 — a caught error variant WITHOUT a `renders` clause falls through to the boundary's `fallback=` markup. loadItem("slow") fails LoadError::Timeout at mount; Timeout has no renders, so eb-fallback shows. The variant-renders markup (eb-notfound) never appears. Siblings survive (§19.6.4).
rationale: §19.6.3 item 2 / §19.6.5 priority #2: when the failing variant has no `renders` clause and the boundary has a `fallback`, the fallback markup is displayed. Timeout (no renders) -> eb-fallback. The fallback being present is exactly what keeps E-ERROR-005 silent (the §19.6.6 exhaustiveness check is satisfied).
